openEHR Description: A generic section header which should be renamed in a template to suit a specific clinical context.
EHDS Definition: The EPS problem section lists and describes clinical problems or conditions currently being monitored for the patient.

openEHR Description: An overall statement of exclusion about all Problems/diagnoses Family history Medications Procedures Adverse reactions or other clinical items that are either not currently present or have not been present in the past.
Overall statement of exclusion of all problems or diagnoses at the time of recording.
openEHR Comment: For example: 'No previous diagnoses' 'No known family history' 'No known history of adverse reactions' 'Never taken any medications' or 'No known operations or significant procedures'.
EHDS Definition: If the section is empty why the list is empty. An empty section typically has some text explaining the empty reason.
MappingNotes: In openEHR, the reason why a problem list is empty can be handled by two separate archetypes (Exclusion - global and Absence of information), whereas in FHIR the reasons are combined in a single valueset.

openEHR Description: Statement that specified health information is not available for inclusion in the health record or extract at the time of recording.
Positive statement that no information is available.
openEHR Comment: For example: No information available about adverse reactions; No information available about problems or diagnoses; No information available about previous procedures performed; or No information available about medications used.

openEHR Description: Description of the reason why there is no information available.
openEHR Comment: For example: patient is unconscious or refuses to provide information. Coding the reason with a terminology is desirable if possible.
EHDS Definition: If the section is empty why the list is empty. An empty section typically has some text explaining the empty reason.
MappingNotes: In openEHR, the reason why a problem list is empty can be handled by two separate archetypes (Exclusion - global and Absence of information), whereas in FHIR the reasons are combined in a single valueset.

openEHR Description: Generic archetype to contain existing CLUSTER archetypes which need to be represented as standalone data.
This cluster is intended to support the recording of FHIR narrative ingested that does not have a more natural target within existing archetypes.
The narrative carried in the div element in FHIR
EHDS Definition: A human-readable narrative that contains the attested content of the section used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it clinically safe for a human to just read the narrative.

openEHR Description: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too) or whether a human authored it and it may contain additional data.
openEHR Comment: FHIR Codesystem: generated | extensions | additional | empty

openEHR Description: Details about a single identified health condition injury disability or any other issue which impacts on the physical mental and/or social well-being of an individual.
openEHR Comment: Clear delineation between the scope of a problem versus a diagnosis is not easy to achieve in practice. For the purposes of clinical documentation with this archetype problem and diagnosis are regarded as a continuum with increasing levels of detail and supportive evidence usually providing weight towards the label of 'diagnosis'.
EHDS Definition: A clinical condition problem diagnosis or other event situation issue or clinical concept that has risen to a level of concern.
MappingNotes: The definitions of the concept mean the same thing, but the openEHR archetype explicitly excludes differential diagnoses, details about pregnancy and assessments about health risk or potential problems.

openEHR Description: Identification of the problem or diagnosis by name.
openEHR Comment: Coding of the name of the problem or diagnosis with a terminology is preferred where possible.
EHDS Definition: The problem code specifies the problem. Depending on the setting different code systems can be used. The ProblemCodelist provides an overview of the possible code systems.
EHDS Binding: The codes SHOULD be taken from Problems - IPS . (preferred to http://hl7.org/fhir/uv/ips/ValueSet/problems-uv-ips) Valueset to describe the actual problem experienced by the patient

openEHR Description: Identification of a simple body site for the location of the problem or diagnosis.
openEHR Comment:
Coding of the name of the anatomical location with a terminology is preferred where possible.
Use this data element to record precoordinated anatomical locations. If the requirements for recording the anatomical location are determined at run-time by the application or require more complex modelling such as relative locations then use the CLUSTER.anatomical_location or CLUSTER.relative_location within the 'Structured anatomical location' SLOT in this archetype. Occurrences for this data element are unbounded to allow for clinical scenarios such as describing a rash in multiple locations but where all of the other attributes are identical. If the anatomical location is included in the Problem/diagnosis name via precoordinated codes this data element becomes redundant.
EHDS Definition: The anatomical location where this condition manifests itself.
EHDS Binding: The codes SHOULD be taken from SNOMEDCTBodyStructures (preferred to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/body-site)

openEHR Description: Estimated or actual date/time that signs or symptoms of the problem/diagnosis were first observed.
openEHR Comment: Data captured/imported as Age at onset should be converted to a date using the subject's date of birth.
EHDS Definition: Onset of the symptom complaint functional limitation complication or date of diagnosis. A â€˜vagueâ€™ date such as only the year or the month and the year is permitted.
MappingNotes: In FHIR, onset is a choice of dateTime, Age, Period, Range or string.

openEHR Description: An assessment of the overall severity of the problem or diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: If severity is included in the Problem/diagnosis name via precoordinated codes this data element becomes redundant. Note: more specific grading of severity can be recorded using the Specific details SLOT.
EHDS Definition: A subjective assessment of the severity of the condition as evaluated by the clinician.
openEHR Values:
Mild local:at0047
 Moderate local:at0048
 Severe local:at0049
-Other Text/CodedText allowed
EHDS Binding: The codes SHOULD be taken from Condition/DiagnosisSeverity (preferred to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-severity)

openEHR Description: Details about findings that support a clinical assertion.
EHDS Definition: Clinical stage or grade of a condition. May include formal severity assessments.

openEHR Description: Identification of an item of clinical evidence by name or type either as a single result or as a grouping of results.
openEHR Comment: It is recommended that 'Evidence' should be coded with a terminology where possible.  For example: 'Mantoux test' 'AFP level' 'tumour volume' 'genetic tests' 'BRCA1 test' 'histopathology result' 'physical examination finding' 'intraoperative finding' 'FIGO stage' 'symptom' 'tumour grading' 'clinical impression'. For use cases where the evidence is sufficiently identified in 'Result' or 'Structured result' this data element may be redundant and has therefore been made optional.
EHDS Definition: The kind of staging such as pathological or clinical staging.
EHDS Binding: For example codes see ConditionStageType (example to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-stage-type|4.0.1) Codes describing the kind of condition staging (e.g. clinical or pathological).

openEHR Description: The result or finding that supports the assertion.
openEHR Comment: For example: '11 mm' '13 Âµg/L' '2.5 ml' or 'BRCA1 positive'. 'Result' can be coded with a terminology where appropriate.
EHDS Definition: A simple summary of the stage such as Stage 3. The determination of the stage is disease-specific.
EHDS Binding: For example codes see ConditionStage (example to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-stage|4.0.1) Codes describing condition stages (e.g. Cancer stages).

openEHR Description: Details about findings that support a clinical assertion.
EHDS Definition: Supporting evidence / manifestations that are the basis of the Condition's verification status such as evidence that confirmed or refuted the condition.

openEHR Description: The result or finding that supports the assertion.
openEHR Comment: For example: '11 mm' '13 Âµg/L' '2.5 ml' or 'BRCA1 positive'. 'Result' can be coded with a terminology where appropriate.
EHDS Definition: A manifestation or symptom that led to the recording of this condition.
EHDS Binding: For example codes see ManifestationAndSymptomCodes (example to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/manifestation-or-symptom|4.0.1) Codes that describe the manifestation or symptoms of a condition.

openEHR Description: Estimated or actual date/time of resolution or remission for this problem or diagnosis as determined by a healthcare professional.
openEHR Comment: Partial dates are acceptable. If the subject of care is under the age of one year then the complete date or a minimum of the month and year is necessary to enable accurate age calculations - for example if used to drive decision support. Data captured/imported as Age at time of resolution should be converted to a date using the subject's date of birth.
EHDS Definition: The date or estimated date that the condition resolved or went into remission. A â€˜vagueâ€™ date such as only the year or the month and the year is permitted
MappingNotes: In FHIR, abatement is a choice of dateTime, Age, Period, Range or string.

openEHR Description: Contextual or temporal qualifier for a specified problem or diagnosis.
Category that supports division of problems and diagnoses into Active or Inactive problem lists.
openEHR Comment: The Active/Inactive and Current/Past data elements have similar clinical impact but represent slightly different semantics. Both are actively used in different clinical settings but usually not together. If a Current/Past qualifier is recorded then this data element is likely to be redundant. An exception where a condition can be current but inactive is asthma that is not causing acute symptoms.
EHDS Definition: The problem status describes the condition of the problem:  Active problems are problems of which the patient experiences symptoms or for which evidence exists.  If condition is abated then clinicalStatus must be either inactive resolved or remission.  Problems with the status 'Inactive' refer to problems that don't affect the patient anymore or that of which there is no evidence of existence anymore.
EHDS Binding: The codes SHALL be taken from ConditionClinicalStatusCodes (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-clinical)
MappingNotes: Clinical status incompletely captures aspects of multiple qualifier axes related to a diagnosis: active/inactive, occurrence (first occurrence/recurrence), remission status (in remission/not in remission), resolution phase.

openEHR Description: Phase of healing for an acute problem or diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: For example: tracking the progress of resolution of a middle ear infection.
EHDS Definition: The problem status describes the condition of the problem:  Active problems are problems of which the patient experiences symptoms or for which evidence exists.  If condition is abated then clinicalStatus must be either inactive resolved or remission.  Problems with the status 'Inactive' refer to problems that don't affect the patient anymore or that of which there is no evidence of existence anymore.
EHDS Binding: The codes SHALL be taken from ConditionClinicalStatusCodes (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-clinical)
MappingNotes: Clinical status incompletely captures aspects of multiple qualifier axes related to a diagnosis: active/inactive, occurrence (first occurrence/recurrence), remission status (in remission/not in remission), resolution phase.

openEHR Description: Status of the remission of an incurable diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: For example: the status of a cancer or haematological diagnosis.
EHDS Definition: The problem status describes the condition of the problem:  Active problems are problems of which the patient experiences symptoms or for which evidence exists.  If condition is abated then clinicalStatus must be either inactive resolved or remission.  Problems with the status 'Inactive' refer to problems that don't affect the patient anymore or that of which there is no evidence of existence anymore.
EHDS Binding: The codes SHALL be taken from ConditionClinicalStatusCodes (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-clinical)
MappingNotes: Clinical status incompletely captures aspects of multiple qualifier axes related to a diagnosis: active/inactive, occurrence (first occurrence/recurrence), remission status (in remission/not in remission), resolution phase.

openEHR Description: Category of the occurrence for this problem or diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: This data element can be an additional qualifier to the 'New' value in the 'Episodicity' value set that is a condition such as asthma can have recurring new episodes that have periods of resolution in between. However it can be important to identify the first ever episode of asthma from all of the other episodes.
EHDS Definition: The problem status describes the condition of the problem:  Active problems are problems of which the patient experiences symptoms or for which evidence exists.  If condition is abated then clinicalStatus must be either inactive resolved or remission.  Problems with the status 'Inactive' refer to problems that don't affect the patient anymore or that of which there is no evidence of existence anymore.
EHDS Binding: The codes SHALL be taken from ConditionClinicalStatusCodes (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-clinical)
MappingNotes: Clinical status incompletely captures aspects of multiple qualifier axes related to a diagnosis: active/inactive, occurrence (first occurrence/recurrence), remission status (in remission/not in remission), resolution phase.

openEHR Description: The level of confidence in the identification of the diagnosis.
openEHR Comment: If an alternative valueset is required these values can be added to the DV_TEXT data type in a template.
EHDS Definition: The verification status to support the clinical status of the condition.
openEHR Values:
Suspected local:at0074
 Probable local:at0075
 Confirmed local:at0076
-Other Text/CodedText allowed
EHDS Binding: The codes SHALL be taken from ConditionVerificationStatus (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-ver-status)
MappingNotes:
Verification status incompletely captures aspects of multiple qualifier axes related to a diagnosis: confirmation, exclusion (refuted), diagnostic process (provisional),
clinical decision-making (differential - one of a list of candidate diagnoses generated during clinical reasoning and used to guide investigation and exclusion). Only 'Confirmed' can be safely mapped between the openEHR and FHIR valuesets at this point in time. There is no clear way of mapping 'Suspected' and 'Probable' to the FHIR values as their meaning can be interpreted and handled differently in different contexts and locations. It is also unclear what the use of ‘refuted’ means in the IPS context and how it should be handled. Differential diagnosis is covered by a different openEHR archetype - Differential diagnoses, which has not been included in the template as it is recommended that differential diagnoses should not be part of the IPS. 'Entered-in-error’ is a record-management state and should result in deletion or retraction in an EHR, not the assignment of condition verification status. For more information, see this openEHR Discourse thread: https://discourse.openehr.org/t/degree-of-certainty-vs-status-in-problem-diagnosis/11561

openEHR Description: Additional narrative about the problem or diagnosis not captured in other fields.
EHDS Definition: Comment by the one who determined or updated the Problem.

openEHR Description: This cluster is intended to support the recording of FHIR narrative ingested that does not have a more natural target within existing archetypes.
The narrative carried in the div element in FHIR
EHDS Definition: Human readable specification of the problem. This is a general notes/comments entry for description of the problem its diagnosis and prognosis.

openEHR Description: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too) or whether a human authored it and it may contain additional data.
openEHR Comment: FHIR Codesystem: generated | extensions | additional | empty
